# runloq — Issue Tracker for AI Coding Agents

> runloq is a local-first SQLite issue tracker designed for AI coding agents (Claude Code, Cursor, Codex). Every ticket carries an `agent` field (which specialist picks it up) and a `model` field (opus/sonnet/haiku), so the backlog is directly executable by your agent. Includes an MCP server, React dashboard, and a Claude Code integration kit.

## What makes it agent-native

- `agent` field: which Claude Code agent persona handles the ticket (frontend-dev, backend-dev, security-engineer, etc.)
- `model` field: which LLM tier runs it (opus / sonnet / haiku) — encodes cost-aware routing at ticket-creation time
- MCP server (`runloq-mcp`): agents read, create, update and close tickets without a UI
- Scheduled + recurring tickets: auto-spawn the next iteration on close with full context link
- Append-only event/audit log: full trace of what the agent did and why
- `blocked_by` auto-cascade: closing a ticket removes it from every dependent ticket's blockers

## Install

```
pipx install runloq
cd your-project
runloq init
runloq serve        # React dashboard at http://localhost:3002
runloq-mcp          # MCP server on stdio
```

## Claude Code integration

One-command install of the full integration kit (CLAUDE.md rules, hooks, skills, agent personas):

```
bash integrations/claude-code/install.sh
```

After install, Claude Code automatically reads the backlog on session start, routes tickets to the right agent+model, and auto-commits runloq.db on session end.

## Key links

- GitHub: https://github.com/runloq/runloq
- MCP docs: https://runloq.github.io/runloq/docs/mcp/
- Claude Code integration: https://runloq.github.io/runloq/docs/claude-code/
- Why runloq?: https://runloq.github.io/runloq/why/

## Alternatives comparison

- vs GitHub Issues: no `agent` field, no `model` field, no MCP server, cloud-only, no local-first SQLite
- vs Linear: designed for human product teams, seat-based pricing, cloud-only, no agent execution model, no MCP server
- vs plain text files: stateless, no audit log, no scheduled tickets, no agent routing

## Use cases

- Solo developer using Claude Code as a "second engineer" — the backlog is what the agent reads each session
- Agent-driven debugging: create a ticket with `agent=backend-dev, model=sonnet`, run `/work SYS-042`, agent executes and closes
- Recurring tasks: weekly code reviews, daily standup prep, monthly invoicing — auto-spawn the next iteration on close
- Multi-agent coordination: orchestrator creates tickets with different agent slugs, dispatches subagents per ticket
